name: sagemaker-ssh-helper

metadata.name: sagemaker-ssh-helper
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: MIT No Attribution License (MIT-0)
metadata.summary: A helper library to connect into Amazon SageMaker with AWS Systems Manager and SSH (Secure Shell)

documentation:
SageMaker SSH Helper is a library that allows you to "SSH into SageMaker", i.e., securely connect to Amazon SageMaker training jobs, processing jobs, and realtime inference endpoints as well as SageMaker Studio notebook containers for fast interactive experimentation, remote debugging, and advanced troubleshooting.

For the documentation, see the repo [https://github.com/aws-samples/sagemaker-ssh-helper/tree/v2.2.1](https://github.com/aws-samples/sagemaker-ssh-helper/tree/v2.2.1).

modules.name: sagemaker_ssh_helper.sm_ssh

Run `sm-ssh -h` for the help.

methods.name: sagemaker_ssh_helper.ide.SSHIDE.create_ssh_kernel_app
documentation:
Creates new kernel app with SSH lifecycle config (see kernel-lc-config.sh ).

Images: <https://docs.aws.amazon.com/sagemaker/latest/dg/notebooks-available-images.html> .

Note that doc is not always up-to-date and doesn't list full names,

: e.g., sagemaker-base-python-310 in the doc is sagemaker-base-python-310-v1 in the CreateApp API .

```
param app_name



param image_name_or_arn

[name] from the images doc above or the full ARN

param instance_type



param ssh_lifecycle_config



param recreate

```
